comments.body: It could happen, I believe. What context are you operating under? / Do you need to use scheduleAtFixedRate, or would scheduleWithFixedDelay work?

@ryanwebjackson yeah, that might be safer, although I was really hoping to have the task kick off as close to every 60 seconds as possible; fixed delay appears to pause in between iterations no matter how long the iteration took.  I guess if this really does cause problems, I'll look into that approach, thanks.
Test it every 1 second and simulate a 2 second delay, with a low heap set and see what happens.
But if each task ends up taking more than 60 seconds, will it still end up being completed and the queue won't overflow

If I'm not mistaken, the ScheduledThreadPoolExecutor implementation only schedules the next execution after the current one completes, regardless of fixed-delay or fixed-rate. So, the queue shouldn't overflow if you get unlucky with a task's execution time. Whether that behavior is guaranteed by the ScheduledExecutorService interface, I'm not sure.

title: Long term behavior of ScheduledExecutorService.scheduleAtFixedRate when tasks run too long
body:
I'm using java.util.concurrent.ScheduledExcecutorService.scheduleAtFixedRate to execute a task periodically. I run it once every minute and it usually completes after 5 seconds, but occasionally runs longer than a minute. The scheduleAtFixedRate service behaves as I'd expect it to, per the Javadoc: "If any execution of this task takes longer than its period, then subsequent executions may start late, but will not concurrently execute." This is the behavior that I want, and I can see that it's working correctly.

That got me wondering, though… If the task is set to queue every 60 seconds and I get really unlucky and every iteration ends up taking longer than 60 seconds, will the work queue eventually fill memory and throw an OutOfMemoryException? I can't find this documented anywhere and the source code does sort of imply that this would be the case. If it is, is there a more robust way to handle this?
